# Orcastrator — Copilot Instructions

Lightweight CLI-first multi-agent coding runtime built on the GitHub Copilot SDK.

## Build & typecheck

```bash
npm run build       # tsc → compiles src/ to dist/
npm run dev         # tsc --watch
npm run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit (use npx tsc --noEmit if tsc isn't on PATH)
```

Tests run via Vitest:

```bash
npm run test        # vitest run (single pass)
npm run test:watch  # vitest (watch mode)
```

## Architecture

```
CLI (src/cli/)  →  Coordinator (src/core/coordinator.ts)
                       │
                   Router (src/core/router.ts)
                   Pattern-matches task text → selects agent(s)
                       │
                   Fan-out (src/core/fan-out.ts)
                   Promise.allSettled → parallel agent spawning
                       │
                   AgentLifecycleManager (src/agents/lifecycle.ts)
                   Compile charter → create session → send task
                       │
                   CopilotClient (src/client/copilot.ts)
                   Singleton wrapper around @github/copilot-sdk
```

**Data flow:**
1. CLI commands in `src/cli/` are thin — they parse args and call `Coordinator.handleTask()`
2. The router matches task text against regex rules → returns agent names
3. `fanOut` spawns all matched agents in parallel via `Promise.allSettled`
4. Each agent receives a compiled charter (its system prompt) built from: agent config + team context + `.orcastrator/decisions.md` + per-agent `.orcastrator/agents/<name>/history.md`
5. Session logs are written to `.orcastrator/log/` as JSON

**Module responsibilities:**
- `src/core/` — Coordinator, router, fan-out, event bus, shared types
- `src/agents/` — Lifecycle manager, charter compiler, model selector
- `src/client/copilot.ts` — Singleton `CopilotClient` wrapper; never instantiate directly
- `src/config/builder.ts` — Public package API (`defineOrcastrator`, `defineAgent`, `defineRouting`); Zod schemas live here
- `src/cli/` — One file per command; delegates all logic to core
- `src/guardrails/` — Permission handler, session hooks, policy enforcement
- `src/skills/` — Skill loader, registry, and types; markdown-based plugins from `.orcastrator/skills/`
- `src/git/` + `src/github/` + `src/linear/` — External integrations
- `src/state/` — Read/write `.orcastrator/` state files
- `src/init/` + `src/config/generator.ts` — `orcastrator init` / `orcastrator build` logic

**`.orcastrator/` directory** (runtime state, committed to git):
- `decisions.md` — shared team decisions injected into every agent's system prompt
- `routing.md` — human-readable routing rules (generated by `orcastrator build`)
- `agents/<name>/charter.md` — agent charter (generated)
- `agents/<name>/history.md` — per-agent learning log
- `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` — markdown-based skill plugins with YAML frontmatter

## Key conventions

### ESM imports require `.js` extensions
NodeNext module resolution is enforced. Always use `.js` in relative imports, even when the source file is `.ts`:
```ts
import { matchRoute } from "./router.js";   // ✅
import { matchRoute } from "./router";      // ✗
```

### Config DSL is the public API
`src/config/builder.ts` is the package's sole export (per `package.json` `exports`). User-facing config is authored with:
```ts
defineOrcastrator({ ... })   // validates entire config via Zod
defineAgent({ ... })
defineRouting({ ... })
```

Agent names must match `/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/` (lowercase, hyphens only).

### Types are inferred from Zod schemas
Core types in `src/core/types.ts` are `z.infer<>` aliases of schemas defined in `src/config/builder.ts`. Add fields to the Zod schema first; the TypeScript type follows automatically.

### CLI commands stay thin
`src/cli/*.ts` files parse CLI args and surface errors — no business logic. Delegate everything to `src/core/`, `src/agents/`, etc.

### CopilotClient is a singleton
`src/client/copilot.ts` manages a single `_client` instance. Use `getClient()` — never `new CopilotClient()` elsewhere. Always call `stopClient()` on shutdown.

### Guardrails composition
`guardrails: true` in config applies defaults; an object is merged with defaults via `normalizeGuardrails()`. Guardrail logic must stay decoupled from core so it can be composed or overridden without touching runtime internals.

### Issue provider auto-detection
`ENG-123` style identifiers → Linear; plain numbers (`42`) → GitHub. Routing logic lives in `src/cli/issue.ts`. Override with `--provider`.

### Secrets
`LINEAR_API_KEY` and `GITHUB_TOKEN` are read from env vars (also loaded from `.env` / `.env.local` at startup in `src/index.ts`). Never commit API keys to `orcastrator.config.ts`.
